description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by two prominent black metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a weathered, natural brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it an aged and rustic appearance. The top is flat and appears to be made from the same wood as the sides, with no visible opening mechanism, suggesting it is sealed. There is a small, dark triangular marking on the side of the barrel, which may serve as a symbol or identifier. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near garages, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a sealed flat top, featuring a small triangular marking on its side. The wood grain and aged finish give it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the barrel, the wood grain, and a clear triangular marking on the center side.
description_view_1: Rotated view with the triangular marking now partially visible on the right, highlighting the curvature and metal hoops.
description_view_2: Side view with the marking less visible, emphasizing the continuous wood staves and the symmetry of the hoops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the triangular marking now on the left, showing the consistent texture and construction.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
